page: Microsoft Azure L8as_v3 is a member of the LASv3 server family, utilizing an AMD EPYC 7763 processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with multithreading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The processor includes 32 KB L1d cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU, and includes 2002 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.

Performance benchmarks indicate a strong single-core CPU capability, with stress-ng single-core performance ranking in the top 25 percent. However, multi-core CPU performance and most Geekbench 5 metrics, such as software compilation, ray tracing, and image processing, fall into the average tier. A notable bottleneck exists in memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, which rank in the bottom 25 percent. Conversely, small-block cached memory reads perform at an average level of 515.46 GB/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the L8as_v3 offers a dense combination of high-capacity local storage and substantial memory relative to its vCPU count. This makes it suitable for storage-heavy and database workloads, such as Redis deployments, which achieved 8.23 million operations per second in testing, and static web serving. It is also well-suited for memory-intensive applications that do not require massive multi-threaded compute scaling, though workloads heavily reliant on large-block memory bandwidth or intensive multi-threaded decompression may experience performance trade-offs.
description: The Microsoft Azure L8as_v3 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the LASv3 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs, 64.0 GB of RAM, and 2002 GB of Premium Storage-capable local storage. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance, though multi-core operations and large-block memory bandwidth are average to weak. With a high 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and substantial local storage, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for database operations, caching layers like Redis, and static web serving. It represents a balanced trade-off for workloads requiring high local storage capacity and memory density without needing top-tier multi-core compute or high-throughput memory bandwidth.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L8as_v3 is an x86_64 server featuring 8 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 2002 GB local storage, offering strong single-core performance for databases and storage-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L8as_v3 features 8 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 2002 GB local storage, optimized for database and memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: An AMD-powered instance featuring dense local storage and high memory capacity optimized for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs at 3.525 GHz
64.0 GB RAM providing a dense 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio
2002 GB of Premium Storage-capable local storage
Strong single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing
Suited for database operations, Redis, and static web serving
